title: Eosin-5’-maleimide (EMA) binding test with fluorecent beads
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3sh2k","text":"The flow cytometry-based eosin-5’-maleimide (EMA) binding test is used for reliable diagnostics of hereditary spherocytosis, a relatively common hereditary anemia. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bl4o2","text":"In this modified version of the EMA-binding test, we utilize commercially available rainbow beads to overcome the the need for three to six healthy controls. However, two healthy controls are still used in this protocol as an extra safety measure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"abgub","text":"The eosin-5’-maleimide (EMA) binding test was performed on blood within 48 hours of sampling. We recommend using EDTA-stabilized blood and store the samples at 4oC until analysis. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":161,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7lppq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ib97","text":"All EMA binding tests were performed on a FACS Canto II (BD Biosciences, Franklin Lakes, NJ, United States) using standard filter options (530/30). Other flow cytometers should do as well. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4474f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"15tk8","text":"This protocol deviates from other EMA-binding tests by utilizing commercially available fluorescent beads instead of three to six healthy control samples.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3mcgs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bseb0","text":"As rainbow bead MFI is slightly higher than the average fluorescence level of healhty controls, one can utilize a constant calibration factor (CF) to make the results comparable to the traditional EMA-binding test. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3cpva","text":"The reason for this CF is solely to ensure that the EMA value obtained can be compared with the EMA values stated in the literature. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"nhav","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ttvg","text":"New lots of rainbow beads should be calibrated towards the previous to ensure consistency of the EMA values calculated. Otherwise, a new CF should be calculated.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c6u32","text":"We rutinely use two healthy controls with each sample as an extra safety measure. We do not age-match these to the patient. If possible, a travel control should be analyzed along with the patient sample. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"38qqg","text":"If normalization to normal controls is desired, the constant calibration factor (CF) should be calculated in advance.     ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"36udb","text":"Treat all human blood as potentially infectious. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dfkvr","text":"Laboratory coats or appropriate gowns must be worn in the laboratory and fastened properly. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d5o0h","text":"Gloves must be worn when handling human blood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e3gp4","text":"All open cuts and abrasions must be covered.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f4gl4","text":"Use disposable equipment wherever possible.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare FACS-tubes for beads: Label 3 tubes for beads and add 500 µl PBS to each. Add 1 drop of beads to each tube (Calibration beads, Blank beads, and rainbow beads). Calibration beads and blank beads are part of a calibration kit from Agilent (#K011011-2) Rainbow beads are puchased separately (BD Biosciences, #556298)
Remove a tube of stock EMA ( 0.5 mg/mL ) aliquot from the freezer to thaw in a dark place @ Room temperature .
Prepare samples
Preparation of stock of EMA-reagent: 5-Maleimido-eosin for fluorescence ≥93% (HPLC) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #63184
Dissolve 10 mg EMA in 20 mL PBS. Mix well.
Prepare 80 new 500 µl PCR tubes by labeling them (MM.YY) Transfer aliquots of 250 µL EMA solution to each tube.
Store in freezer. Aliquots of frozen dissolved EMA dye should be stable for at least six months. Aliquots should be discarded after use (i.e. not frozen agin for later use).

Label a 14 ml tube for each sample 10 mL 0.9% NaCl is added to each tube 100 µL of mixed blood is added to its corresponding tube Mix/Vortex tubes 1500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes Discard supernatant
Wash twice: Add 10 mL 0.9% NaCl Mix/Vortex 1500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes Discard supernatant
Transfer 5 µL of washed packed red cells to a new (labelled) 1.5 mL eppendorf tube
Add 25 µL of EMA stock solution Incubate for 01:00:00 @ Room temperature, in a dark place Mix every 00:15:00
1500 x g, Room temperature, 30 seconds Carefully discard supernatant
Add 500 µL PBS Mix/Vortex
Prepare FACS tubes by adding labels Transfer 25 µL of labelled red cells to the new tube
Wash the labelled RBC's 3 times: Add 3 mL PBS with 0.5% BSA Mix/Vortex 1500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes Discard supernatant
Samples are now ready to be run on the flow cytometer.

For each patient sample: Start running the Calibration beads and adjust the voltage of the PMT in such a way that the fluorescence FL1 is approximately 50,000 (or as close hereto as possible). Acquire 15,000 events. Run Blank beads and acquire 15,000 events. Run Rainbow beads and acquire 15,000 events. Run Control samples and acquire 15,000 events. Run Patient sample and acquire 15,000 events.
Setup flow cytometer (Canto II): Set it to LOG-mode for FSC, SSC, and FL1 Apply a threshold of 5000 for the FSC.
The analysis of the results can be done differently.
Add 500 µL PBS with 0.5% BSA Mix/Vortex
creator: Andreas Glenthoej
